commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a non-streaming reply-part combiner, uses it for final and `/btw` gateway reply recombination, and adds regression tests for split indentation, authored newline boundaries, and CRLF trimming.

PR surface: Source +17, Tests +79. Total +96 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main still trims `/btw`, final display, and final transcript payload chunks before recombining them. I did not run tests because this was a read-only review, but the code path directly matches the reported split-payload indentation loss.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A repair worker can make the narrow mechanical refresh onto current main, with maintainer review still needed for the accepted visible spacing behavior.

target.title: fix(gateway): preserve indentation when recombining non-streaming replies
target.body:
AI-assisted: yes (OpenClaw agent-authored).
Testing: targeted local validation (gateway regression tests + targeted lint + pre-commit repo checks).

## Summary

- Companion to merged #55960, not a duplicate: #55960 fixed indentation loss while stripping inline reply directives in `src/utils/directive-tags.ts`; this PR fixes the remaining gateway-layer loss when non-streaming reply parts are recombined in `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts`.
- Preserve each non-streaming payload chunk as authored when recombining it, so indentation-sensitive content survives payload boundaries.
- Reuse that same combiner for `/btw` side results so sibling non-streaming gateway paths behave consistently.
- Normalize `CRLF` / `CR` / `LF` boundary trimming and add focused regression coverage for the single-newline, `/btw`, and CRLF cases.
- Scope boundary: no streaming-path formatting changes, no Markdown parsing changes, and no channel-specific formatting rules.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #42576
- Related #29377
- Companion to merged #55960

## Relationship to #55960

Merged PR #55960 fixed one real whitespace-loss layer: inline reply-directive stripping in the shared parser (`src/utils/directive-tags.ts`).

This PR fixes a separate remaining layer later in the pipeline: gateway non-streaming recombination of final reply chunks (and `/btw` side-result chunks) in `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts`.

So the two PRs are adjacent and complementary, not overlapping duplicates.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Non-streaming final replies and `/btw` side results now preserve indentation when formatted content spans multiple payloads. When one chunk already ends with a single newline and the next chunk does not start with one, the gateway now preserves that authored single newline instead of forcing a blank line. The combiner also trims `CRLF` / `CR` / `LF` cleanly at outer boundaries so Windows-style line endings do not leak stray carriage returns.

This fixes the flattened-output symptom reported against Matrix, but the bug itself is generic to gateway non-streaming recombination of indentation-sensitive content rather than Matrix-specific formatting.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local Node.js checkout
- Model/provider: n/a (gateway-side reply recombination)
- Integration/channel (if any): reproduced against the gateway non-streaming `chat.send` path; user report was on Matrix
- Relevant config (redacted): n/a

### Steps

1. Produce a non-streaming assistant reply whose formatted block is emitted across multiple final payloads.
2. Include indentation-sensitive content such as nested YAML, a nested list, or a fenced code block.
3. Observe the stored/broadcast final message text.

### Expected

- Indentation from each payload chunk is preserved in the final combined reply.

### Actual

- Leading whitespace at payload boundaries was trimmed away, flattening structured content.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Added focused regression coverage for:
- the authored single-newline separator case
- an indentation-sensitive `/btw` YAML side result
- `CRLF` boundary trimming

The linked user issue includes before/after screenshots of the real-world symptom.

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: targeted gateway regression test for the single-newline recombination edge case; targeted `/btw` side-result regression test for an indented YAML block; targeted `CRLF` boundary regression coverage; targeted lint on the changed files; repo pre-commit checks during commit.
- Edge cases checked: whitespace-only payload chunks are still ignored; separator insertion still only happens when adjacent chunks are not already separated by line breaks; authored single-newline separators are preserved instead of expanded to blank lines; `CRLF` boundaries do not leave stray carriage returns.
- What you did **not** verify: live Matrix delivery against a running instance, broader streaming reply behavior outside the existing `/btw` block-side-result coverage, or full-repo test suites.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert commits `6b2461a11`, `c753cd0af`, and `d667f18f1` (or revert the PR branch changes as a unit)
- Files/config to restore: `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts`, and `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: unexpected extra blank lines between merged payload chunks, unexpected loss of an authored single newline between chunks, stray carriage returns from `CRLF` boundaries, or lost indentation in `/btw` side results

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: preserving authored separators changes the old trim/join behavior for payloads that already end in a single newline.
  - Mitigation: this is intentional, explicitly regression-tested, and still bounded by the same whitespace-only-chunk skipping plus separator insertion only when neither side already provides a line break.
- Risk: preserving chunk text could expose `CRLF` edge cases at outer boundaries.
  - Mitigation: the combiner now trims `CRLF` / `CR` / `LF` consistently at the start/end, with targeted regression coverage.
- Risk: reviewers may mistake this PR for a duplicate of #55960.
  - Mitigation: this description now names the exact layer split: `#55960` fixes directive parsing, this PR fixes gateway non-streaming recombination.
target.author: vrurg

commands.author: AScriver

target.title: fix(discord): preserve target intent during normalization
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Discord-like explicit DM targets could be normalized through the id-like target path without preserving the intended direct-message kind.
- Why it matters: A Discord target that is syntactically a user/direct target should stay routed as a user/direct target instead of falling back to generic group/channel inference.
- What changed: Shared target-kind inference now lives in `target-normalization`, and both outbound target resolution and cron delivery pass the inferred kind into id-like plugin resolution.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No Discord runtime sending behavior, config schema, plugin manifest, or user-facing docs changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes N/A
- Related N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Explicit Discord direct-message targets such as `user:<id>` preserve inferred user/direct intent when id-like target resolution runs for outbound and cron delivery.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Windows/PowerShell, Discord plugin configured with a real bot token and a redacted explicit `user:<id>` target.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran local OpenClaw Discord outbound/cron delivery paths against an explicit `user:<id>` Discord target using the patched branch, then compared the live delivery result with the new resolver/cron coverage.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Existing live-result report from @AScriver after the local Discord run, with private IDs and tokens redacted:

```text
Scenario: Discord outbound/cron delivery to an explicit user:<id> target
Result: delivered to the intended DM recipient and preserved explicit user target intent instead of falling back to generic group/channel inference
Coverage alignment: matches the targeted resolver and cron tests added in this PR
```

- Observed result after fix: Discord delivered to the intended DM recipient, and the explicit user/direct target intent was preserved instead of falling back to generic group/channel inference.
- What was not tested: Raw Discord token and private IDs are not published; no additional non-Discord channels were tested live.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Source-level before-state showed the id-like resolver did not receive inferred `preferredKind` for outbound/cron paths; the targeted regression tests cover that missing handoff.
## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: id-like target resolution received only the raw target string, so the plugin resolver could lose the caller's inferred direct-message intent after normalization.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered id-like fallback and directory inference separately, but not propagation of inferred DM/user kind into plugin id-like resolution.
- Contributing context (if known): Discord target parsing can distinguish user/direct targets before the generic id-like normalization path runs.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/infra/outbound/target-resolver.test.ts`, `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: inferred direct-message/user kind is passed into id-like plugin target resolution.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it validates the shared resolver seam and cron delivery call path without needing a live Discord bot.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Discord-targeted delivery paths preserve explicit direct-message intent more reliably when resolving id-like targets. No config or command syntax changes.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Discord DM-like target -> generic id-like resolution -> kind may default away from user/direct intent

After:
Discord DM-like target -> infer user/direct kind -> id-like resolution receives preferredKind=user -> direct target preserved
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Windows, PowerShell
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout, Node/pnpm from workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord target routing logic
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Resolve a plugin id-like target whose channel messaging adapter infers `direct` chat type.
2. Resolve a cron delivery target for an explicit DM-like target.
3. Assert the id-like resolver receives `preferredKind: "user"` and preserves the returned target.

### Expected

- The id-like plugin resolver receives the inferred user/direct kind.
- Cron delivery preserves explicit DM intent after target normalization.

### Actual

- Verified by the new focused tests.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Targeted validation:

```bash
pnpm test src/infra/outbound/target-resolver.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.test.ts
pnpm check:changed
```

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: local Discord outbound/cron delivery to a redacted explicit `user:<id>` target was reported after this patch; target resolver and cron delivery target unit/seam coverage also passed locally.
- Edge cases checked: id-like raw targets continue passing `preferredKind: "group"`; explicit DM-like targets pass `preferredKind: "user"`.
- What I did **not** verify: raw Discord token/private IDs are intentionally not published; no additional non-Discord channels were tested live.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: shared target kind inference could affect non-Discord channels that expose `inferTargetChatType`.
  - Mitigation: the helper preserves existing fallback behavior, maps `channel` to `group` where directory lookup expects groups, and adds focused coverage for the new id-like propagation path.
